sections_cited: sections 36 and 37 of the Electronic Transactions Ordinance

chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Crl. Rev. Application No. 53 of 2023 Crl. Rev. Application No. 54 of 2023 Crl. Rev. Application No. 55 of 2023 Crl. Rev. Application No. 56 of 2023 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGES For hearing of main case 17-03-2023 Mr. Haider Waheed and Mr. Shahzeb Akhtar and Mr. Fahad Ali Hashmi, Advocates for the applicant in Crl. Rev. No.53/2023. M/s. Abid S. Zuberi, Ayyan Mustafa Memon, Easha Azmat, and Yar Muhammad Maitlo, Advocates for the applicant in Crl.Rev. No.54/2023. M/s. Anwar Mansoor Khan, Asim Mansoor Khan, Umaima Anwar Khan and Muhammad Zaheer, Advocates for the applicant in Crl. Rev. Nos. 55 & 56 of 2023. Mr. Muhammad Ahmed, Assistant Attorney General. ============= Omar Sial, J: Adnan Saboor (in Criminal Revision App. No. 53 of 2023), Ms. Ayesha Shoaib (in Criminal Revision App. No. 54 of 2023), Imran Ahmed Khan (in Criminal Revision App. No. 55 of 2023) and Viven Christopher (in Criminal Revision App. No. 56 of 2023) have impugned orders dated 20.02.2023 and 21.02.2023 respectively passed by the learned 8th Additional Sessions Judge, Karachi South. In terms of the said orders section 265-K Cr.P.C. applications filed by these persons were dismissed. 2. Background relevant for the present purpose is that all applicants are nominated accused in a case arising out of F.I.R. No. 07 of 2015 registered at the F.I.A.’s Corporate Crime Circle. The case was registered on 27.06.2015 on behalf of the State. It was alleged that the then Chief Executive Officer of a company by the name of Axact (Private) Limited, a Mr. Shoaib Shaikh, along with the applicants herein were guilty of offences pursuant to sections 420, 468, 471, 472, 473, 474, 477-A, 109 and 34 P.P.C.

It was alleged that the then Chief Executive Officer of a company by the name of Axact (Private) Limited, a Mr. Shoaib Shaikh, along with the applicants herein were guilty of offences pursuant to sections 420, 468, 471, 472, 473, 474, 477-A, 109 and 34 P.P.C. 2 as well as sections 3 and 4 of the Anti-Money Laundering Act, 2010 as well as sections 36 and 37 of the Electronic Transactions Ordinance, 2010. Mr. Shoaib Shaikh was alleged to be the mastermind of the crime and the applicants and some others, who were all members of Axact’s management team, except Ms. Ayesha Shaikh, who was the wife of Mr. Shoaib Shaikh, facilitated, aided and abetted, Mr. Shoaib Shaikh in the alleged wrong doing. 3. It is a matter of record that Mr. Shoaib Shaikh and some other members of the management team with similar allegations as on the present applicants were acquitted by this Court vide judgment dated 10.3.2023. The learned Assistant Attorney General agrees that as the allegations against the present applicants are the same, and as Mr. Shaikh himself has been acquitted, the same benefit must also be extended to the present applicants. 4. Accordingly, for the same reasons as contained in the judgment dated 10-3-2023, the applicants herein are acquitted of the charge. They are all on bail. There bail bonds stand cancelled and sureties discharged. JUDGE
